mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances following a >0.5pp unemployment rise over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns during expansion periods with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPI exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold across multiple inflation cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic growth and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles with p-values above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.5% monthly industrial production decline over a 10-year backtest.
